channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Alzheimer’s may begin with a silent drop in brain blood flow
item.description: Subtle changes in brain blood flow and oxygen use are closely linked to hallmark signs of Alzheimer’s, including amyloid plaques and memory-related br...

item.title: Something strange is happening in the Milky Way’s magnetic field
item.description: Deep inside the Milky Way, an invisible force is quietly holding everything together — its magnetic field. Now, researchers have created one of the mo...

item.title: Congo basin blackwater lakes are releasing ancient carbon into the atmosphere
item.description: Deep in the Congo Basin, vast peatlands quietly store enormous amounts of Earth’s carbon — but new research suggests this ancient vault may be leaking...

item.title: Can solar storms trigger earthquakes? Scientists propose surprising link
item.description: Scientists have proposed a surprising connection between solar flares and earthquakes. When solar activity disturbs the ionosphere, it may generate el...

item.title: 50 year quest ends with creation of silicon aromatic once thought impossible
item.description: After nearly 50 years of failed attempts and scientific speculation, chemists at Saarland University have achieved what many thought might be impossib...

item.title: NSF to proceed with demolition and site restoration at Sacramento Peak Observatory
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced today it will proceed with demolition and site restoration at the Sacramento Peak Observatory, also kno...

item.title: NSF launches $100M National Quantum and Nanotechnology Research Infrastructure program
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation is investing up to $100 million to establish a nationwide network of open-access research facilities for quantum ...

item.title: NSF announces first AI-ENGAGE awards to modernize global agriculture
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation, in coordination with partner agencies from Australia, India and Japan, today announced the first cohort of award...

item.title: Transitioning NCAR-Wyoming Supercomputing Center operations
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation has informed the NSF National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) that management and operations of the NCAR-W...

item.title: NSF and partners invest $9M in AI-focused math education program
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation Directorate for Social, Behavioral, and Economic Sciences (NSF SBE) has launched the Collaboratory to Advance Mat...
